events.title: Aavegotchi whitepaper + GHST private sale
events.summary: Pixelcraft Studios published the Aavegotchi whitepaper and ran a private GHST sale (5M GHST at 0.05 DAI), establishing the DeFi-staked NFT concept and seeding initial capital.

events.title: GHST token launch via Aragon bonding-curve presale
events.summary: GHST went live on Ethereum mainnet through an Aragon Fundraising continuous bonding curve opening at 0.2 DAI/GHST, the first public way to acquire GHST and the project's core funding mechanism.

events.title: AavegotchiDAO inaugural vote (DAICO tap)
events.summary: The first AavegotchiDAO proposal (Aragon DAICO) opened Oct 15 and passed ~99%, raising the core-team funding tap from 50K to 100K DAI/month and starting GHST on-chain governance.

events.title: First GHST staking raffle (Chainlink wearables)
events.summary: The first of three pre-mainnet staking raffles distributed LINK-themed wearables (6,010 prizes), debuting the FRENS-to-ticket mechanic and the Chainlink VRF partnership for random traits.

events.title: Aave invests in Pixelcraft Studios
events.summary: Aave publicly announced a stake in Pixelcraft Studios (Singapore), formalizing the relationship behind the 'Aave' in Aavegotchi, alongside backers IOSG and Noir Ventures.

events.title: Jan 4 Ethereum mainnet launch announced
events.summary: Aavegotchi announced a planned Ethereum mainnet 'Haunt 1' launch for Jan 4, 2021 with 10,000 Portals, generating major coverage and setting up the eventual Polygon pivot.

events.title: Pivot to Polygon (Matic) announced
events.summary: After extreme Ethereum gas fees on the planned launch day, the team announced Aavegotchi would deploy on Polygon/Matic to make frequent gameplay transactions free and instant.

events.title: GHST live on Polygon + liquidity migration
events.summary: GHST went live on Matic (QuickSwap) with $50K+ in liquidity-migration rewards, completing the token's move to Polygon ahead of the game launch.

events.title: Baazaar marketplace announced
events.summary: Pixelcraft announced the Baazaar, a native on-chain marketplace for Gotchis, Portals and wearables, launching alongside Haunt 1 and becoming a core economic hub.

events.title: Snapshot governance + first signaling vote
events.summary: The governance forum and Snapshot signaling launched, with the first community vote deciding max Portals per transaction, beginning the off-chain AGIP era.

events.title: Mainnet launch on Polygon (Haunt 1)
events.summary: Aavegotchi went live March 2 with 10,000 Haunt 1 summoning Portals on Polygon, the true mainnet launch and first time players could mint and summon Gotchis.

events.title: Rarity Farming Season 1 begins
events.summary: The inaugural Rarity Farming season (Apr 20 to Jun 15) distributed a 1.4M GHST pool by rarity/kinship/XP, establishing Aavegotchi's recurring play-to-earn loop.

events.title: Gotchi Gaatherer: first playable mini-game
events.summary: Community dev BitFlicker released Gotchi Gaatherer, Aavegotchi's first playable game, demonstrating the player-built mini-game ecosystem the project aimed to seed.

events.title: First GBM Bid-to-Earn auction
events.summary: Aavegotchi ran its first GBM Bid-to-Earn auction for ERC-1155 wearables, where outbid users earn a share of incentives, introducing a novel NFT distribution mechanic.

events.title: Bid-to-Earn auctions opened to anyone
events.summary: Any user could now launch their own Bid-to-Earn GBM auctions in the Aauction House, decentralizing NFT distribution beyond the core team.

events.title: First Aavegotchi Game Jaam
events.summary: Aavegotchi held its first Game Jaam (Sept 1-5), a hackathon to build NFT mini-games using Gotchi assets, kicking off the community-game-dev push.

events.title: First gameplay Alpha + Gotchi Lending
events.summary: Aavegotchi shipped its first gameplay alpha alongside Gotchi Lending (renting out Gotchis for rarity-farming yield), an early step toward Gotchiverse gameplay.
